name: Floyd's Fast & Slow Pointers
summary: Advance a slow pointer one node and a fast pointer two nodes each step; if they ever meet there is a cycle, and O(1) extra memory is used.
visualizationIntent: init: build the linked list from the values, wiring each node to the next and linking the tail back to pos when there is a cycle (a visible back-edge); slow and fast both start at the head. loop: each step fast hops two nodes and slow hops one, both pointers gliding along the chain. check: after each move, compare whether fast and slow landed on the same node. update: if they coincide, flash that node and report a cycle; if fast runs off the end (reaches the null sentinel) the loop ends with no cycle. return: show the boolean answer.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many nodes the list has.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty next-pointer array (nxt[i] = the id the i-th node points to).
lineExplanations.5: Loop over every node to wire up its next pointer.
lineExplanations.6: For every node except the last, the next node is simply the following index.
lineExplanations.7: Point node i at node i+1.
lineExplanations.9: The tail points at pos — the cycle target, or -1 for no cycle.
lineExplanations.10: slow starts at the head (id 0); -1 marks 'no node' for an empty list.
lineExplanations.11: fast also starts at the head — both pointers begin together.
lineExplanations.12: found records the answer; it stays False until the pointers meet.
lineExplanations.13: Keep going while fast has a node and a next node to hop to.
lineExplanations.14: Move fast two nodes ahead (nxt of nxt).
lineExplanations.16: If fast and slow now sit on the same node, they've collided inside a cycle.
lineExplanations.17: Record that a cycle was found.
lineExplanations.18: Stop early — the answer is settled.
lineExplanations.19: Return whether a cycle was found.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def hasCycle(self, values, pos): values are the node values; pos is the cycle target index (-1 = none).
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops i over each node index.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i < n - 1: true for every node except the last.
syntaxExplanations.7: nxt.append(i + 1) records that node i points to node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.8: else: handles the final node (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.9: nxt.append(pos) records the tail's next as pos (the cycle link, or -1).
syntaxExplanations.10: slow = 0 if n > 0 else -1 — a ternary; -1 is the null sentinel for an empty list.
syntaxExplanations.11: fast = 0 if n > 0 else -1 — fast also starts at the head.
syntaxExplanations.12: found = False initializes the boolean answer.
syntaxExplanations.13: while fast != -1 and nxt[fast] != -1: fast needs both a node and a next to take two hops.
syntaxExplanations.14: fast = nxt[nxt[fast]] hops fast forward by two nodes.
syntaxExplanations.15: slow = nxt[slow] hops slow forward by one node.
syntaxExplanations.16: if fast == slow: are both pointers on the same node?
syntaxExplanations.17: found = True marks that the pointers met — a cycle exists.
syntaxExplanations.19: return found hands back the boolean answer.
